source.title: BSEE Gulf of Mexico Region Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Incident

event.top_event: Eye injury from completion fluid splash
actions_taken: Both crewmembers sent for medical evaluation
One crewmember received first aid and released to full duty
Second crewmember received prescription medication and advised to rest eye with follow-up doctor visits
event.summary: On 25-OCT-2014, during well completion operations on East Cameron 321A platform, a well U-Tubed forcing completion fluid out of the tubing. The fluid splashed on two crewmembers' eyes. Both were wearing basic PPE (safety glasses, rubber boots, impact rubber gloves) but not the required chemical goggles and face shield specified in the Zinc Bromide Safety Data Sheet for splashing hazards. One crewmember received first aid only; the second received medical care and was advised to rest and follow up with an eye doctor.
recommendations: Lake Charles District office has no recommendations at this time
key_phrases: chemical goggles and face shield not worn

hazards.name: Chemical exposure (Zinc Bromide, Sodium Chloride, Potassium Chloride)

consequences.name: Chemical splash to eyes of crewmembers
Eye injury requiring medical evaluation and treatment
Required evacuation and loss of work time

controls.name: Chemical goggles and face shield for splashing hazards
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to prevent chemical contact with eyes
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense against chemical splash injury

supporting_text: Both crewmembers were not wearing proper personal protection equipment, chemical goggles and face shield where splashing hazard existed
The Safety Data sheet for the handling of Zinc Bromide states 'Chemical goggles; also wear a face shield if splashing hazard exists' for eye protection
Both crewmembers were wearing personal protection equipment, safety glasses, rubber boots, and impact rubber gloves

controls.name: Safety Data Sheet (SDS) requirements for chemical handling
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to communicate hazards and required PPE

supporting_text: The Safety Data sheet for the handling of Zinc Bromide states 'Chemical goggles; also wear a face shield if splashing hazard exists' for eye protection

controls.name: Well control procedures during packer removal
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent well U-Tubing

supporting_text: The completion crew was in the process of removing the 7.625" production packer set at 5390 feet True Vertical Depth when the well U-Tubed forcing completion fluid out of the tubing
U-Tubing of the well forced completion fluid out of the tubing

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
